articles.title: Ya Allah reham! Ayesha Omar reacts to rising heatwave in Punjab - 24 News HD
articles.full_text:
Ya Allah reham! Ayesha Omar reacts to rising heatwave in Punjab
Lollywood actress Ayesha Omar has stepped forward to amplify a critical weather warning urging citizens to prioritize their health and safety amid rising temperatures.
Taking to Instagram, the actress reshared a heatwave alert for the province, adding her own heartfelt message of concern. “Allah Reham! Stay hydrated with lemon and salts folk. And stay indoors as much as possible,” she wrote, emphasizing the seriousness of the situation.
Her advice highlights simple yet essential precautions encouraging people to drink water mixed with lemon and salts (a homemade electrolyte solution) to combat dehydration, and to limit outdoor exposure to avoid heatstroke and exhaustion. With temperatures climbing to dangerous levels across parts of Punjab, such reminders have struck a chord with her followers.
The post quickly gained attention online, with many users appreciating her for using her platform to spread awareness during extreme weather conditions.
Meanwhile, on the professional front, Ayesha Omar is gearing up for the release of her upcoming film Mera Lyari. Written and directed by Abu Aleeha, the film is scheduled for a nationwide release on May 8, 2026, under the banner of Distribution Club.
Mera Lyari sets itself apart as a sports drama that challenges long standing stereotypes about Lyari. Instead of focusing on its troubled past, the film celebrates its vibrant football culture and the resilience of young women striving to pursue their dreams against societal odds.
Featuring a strong ensemble cast including Dananeer Mobeen and Trinette Lucas, alongside seasoned performers like Sammiya Mumtaz, Nayyar Ejaz, and Adnan Shah Tipu, the film promises a compelling narrative filled with emotion, grit, and purpose.
